messages.content:
The breakdown of organic compounds to produce ATP is known as
homeostasis
cellular respiration
transpiration
 
photosynthesis
dehydration synthesis
The breakdown of organic compounds to produce ATP is known as cellular respiration.

When living cells break down molecules of food, energy is
released as DNA
stored as ADP
created by the blood
 
created in the nucleus
stored as ATP
When living cells break down molecules of food, energy is stored as ATP.

In order to produce the most ATP, an organism needs to perform
photosynthesis
hematopoiesis
dehydration synthesis
 
aerobic cellular respiration
anaerobic cellular respiration
In order to produce the most ATP, an organism needs to perform aerobic cellular respiration.

Energy is released from ATP when the bond is broken between
adenine and ribose
ribose and a phosphate group
ribose and deoxyribose
 
adenine and a phosphate group
two phosphate groups
Energy is released from ATP when the bond is broken between the second and third phosphate groups (ribose and a phosphate group).

When games and competitions are held at high altitudes, athletes must specially train to postpone their cells from switching to
anaerobic cellular respiration
dehydration synthesis
aerobic cellular respiration
 
photosynthesis
hematopoiesis
When games and competitions are held at high altitudes, athletes must specially train to postpone their cells from switching to anaerobic cellular respiration.

What type of respiration needs oxygen?

Lactic acid fermentation


Anaerobic

 

Aerobic


Alcohol fermentation
Aerobic respiration requires oxygen.

During glycolysis, glucose is broken down into two _____.

Electrons


Pyruvate molecules

 

Lactic acid molecules


Ethanol molecules
During glycolysis, glucose is broken down into two pyruvate molecules.

Which of the following produces ethanol?

Alcohol fermentation


Lactic acid fermentation

 

Aerobic respiration


Photosynthesis
Alcohol fermentation produces ethanol.

What is created during aerobic respiration?

Water, carbon dioxide, and ATP


Ethanol, carbon dioxide, and ATP

 

Glucose, oxygen, and ATP


Oxygen, water, and ATP
During aerobic respiration, water, carbon dioxide, and ATP are created.

How much ATP is produced in aerobic respiration?

36


0

 

2


34
In aerobic respiration, a net gain of 36 ATP molecules is produced.

Which of the following chemical reactions accurately describes the reduction of NAD during cellular respiration?
NAD + 2H --> NADH2
NADH + H+ --> NAD+ + 2H
 
NAD+ + 2H --> NADH + H+
None of these answers are correct.

The correct chemical reaction that describes the reduction of NAD during cellular respiration is:
NAD+ + 2H --> NADH + H+.

Which of the following represents the oxidized form of flavin adenine dinucleotide during cellular respiration?

FADH + H+


FADH2

 

FAD+


FAD
The oxidized form of flavin adenine dinucleotide during cellular respiration is FAD.

Which of the following statements about FAD are true?

I. FAD plays a role in cellular respiration.

II. FADH2 is the oxidized form of FAD.

III. FAD can accept electrons.

I, II, and III
II
 
I and III
I and II

The correct statement is: 

I. FAD plays a role in cellular respiration.
